methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $44M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Glyphic Biotechnologies is a Series A biotech company founded by Joshua Yang and Daniel Estandian that has raised $44M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public-market biotech firms (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers at Series B and adjacent stages, including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two quantitative methods are applicable. The stage-progression approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to seed/total-raised progression, yielding $88M. The peer-median method anchors to the 6-company median valuation of $805M across same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, with a range from $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median 7.4x across 3 tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $75M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $446M. The wide dispersion reflects structural uncertainty: stage-progression methods are conservative, relying only on capital deployed, while peer-median relies on comparable companies spanning pre-commercial to revenue-generating stages. Key uncertainties center on clinical/regulatory milestones and revenue traction relative to the peer set.
